title: ETIRACETAM
description: Etiracetam is a pyrrolidinone derivative and an analog of piracetam that functions as a nootropic agent and seizure medication. It is primarily recognized for its ability to facilitate memory retrieval and is the parent compound of the potent antiepileptic drug levetiracetam.

research.title: Memory retrieval deficits: Alleviation by etiracetam, a nootropic drug
research.summary: This study demonstrated that etiracetam facilitates memory retrieval in rats across various experimental models when administered shortly before testing.

research.title: Effect of Levetiracetam on Cognition: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Double-Blind Randomized Placebo-Controlled Trials
research.summary: Analyses of the etiracetam derivative levetiracetam show significant improvements in executive function and cognitive performance in patients with and without epilepsy.

sentiment.summary: Users generally view etiracetam as a mild cognitive enhancer that is often overshadowed by its more potent derivatives like levetiracetam or phenylpiracetam.

effects.label: Retrieval Focus:
effects.description: Most effective at assisting the 'recall' phase of memory rather than initial learning.

effects.label: Nootropic Potency:
effects.description: Considered less potent than modern racetams but with a very high safety profile.

effects.label: Synergistic Potential:
effects.description: Often reported to work best when used in conjunction with a choline source.

dosage.label: Standard Dose:
dosage.description: Typically researched in ranges comparable to piracetam, often 800-1600mg.

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Most effective when taken approximately 30-60 minutes before cognitive tasks.

dosage.label: Frequency:
dosage.description: Usually administered 1-2 times daily due to its metabolic half-life.

sideEffects.label: Headaches:
sideEffects.description: Commonly reported if used without supplemental choline.

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Distress:
sideEffects.description: Minor nausea or stomach upset in sensitive users.

sideEffects.label: Irritability:
sideEffects.description: Occasional reports of mild emotional agitation, similar to 'racetam rage'.

availability.label: Legal Status:
availability.description: Generally sold as a research chemical or dietary supplement in various jurisdictions.

availability.label: Sourcing:
availability.description: Primarily found through specialized nootropic vendors; less common than levetiracetam.
